zh.lifeSummary: 當Gérard IV de Metz de Bouzonville於0981年出生在法國時，他的父親Adalbert II comte de Metz et de Saargau22歲，母親Judith von Öhningen32歲。他於大約1008年與Gisèle結婚。他們至少是6個兒子和4個女兒的父母。他去世於1047年4月4日在法國洛萊沃斯格斯雷米雷蒙特享年66歲，埋葬在法國洛萊莫瑟勒布左維爾。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Gérard IV de Metz de Bouzonville于0981年出生在法国时，他的父亲Adalbert II comte de Metz et de Saargau22岁，母亲Judith von Öhningen32岁。他于大约1008年与Gisèle结婚。他们至少是6个儿子和4个女儿的父母。他去世于1047年4月4日在法国洛萊沃斯格斯雷米雷蒙特享年66岁，埋葬在法国洛萊莫瑟勒布左維爾。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.
Indian: variant of   Dey  .

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Metz
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: German: from a short form of the female personal name Mechthild, composed of ancient Germanic maht ‘might, strength’ + hild ‘strife, battle’.

German, French (Alsace and Lorraine), and Jewish (Ashkenazic): habitational name from Metz in Lorraine, which took its name from a Gaulish tribe, the Mediomatrici; in the name of the place this became abbreviated to Mettis, hence the modern name.
German: from Mätz, a short form of the personal name Matthias (see Matthew ).

variants.name: Mette
meanings: North German: from a pet form of the female personal name Mechthild (see Metz ).
North German: occupational name for a miller, from Middle Low German matte, mette ‘dry measure’.
French: topographic name from Old French mete ‘boundary stone, boundary’ (from Latin meta ‘bounded estate’).

variants.name: Metzer
meanings: German and Jewish (Ashkenazic): habitational name for someone from Metz (see   Metz  ).
Dutch: occupational name for a bricklayer, Middle Dutch metsere.

variants.name: Metts
meanings: Americanized form of German   Metz  .

variants.name: Matthew
meanings: English and Scottish: from the Middle English personal name Ma(t)thew, a vernacular form of the Greek New Testament name Matthias, Matthaios, which is ultimately from the Hebrew personal name Matityahu ‘gift of God’. This was taken into Latin as Mat(t)hias and Matthaeus respectively, the former being used for the twelfth apostle (who replaced Judas Iscariot) and the latter for the author of the first Gospel. In many European languages this distinction is reflected in different name forms. The commonest vernacular forms of the personal name, including English Matthew, Old French Matheu, Spanish Mateo, Italian Matteo, Portuguese Mateus, Catalan and Occitan Mateu are generally derived from the form Matthaeus. In North America, the English form of the surname has absorbed cognates from other languages, including German Matthias , Greek Mathias and Mattheos, Assyrian/Chaldean Matti , Czech Matěj, Slovak, Croatian, and Slovenian Matej . The name Matthew is also found among Christians in southern India (compare Mathai ), but since South Indians traditionally do not have hereditary surnames, the southern Indian name was in most cases registered as such only after immigration of its bearers to the US. Compare Mathew , see also Matthews .

variants.name: Metze
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Armin, Gerhard, Gernot.
German:
metronymic from a short form of the personal name Mechthild (see Metz 1).

nameMeanings.name: Gérard
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Old French name of Germanic (Frankish) origin, introduced to Britain by the Normans. It is derived from gār, gēr ‘spear’ + hard ‘brave, hardy, strong’. In the later Middle Ages this was a more common name than Gerald , with which it was sometimes confused. Nowadays it is less common, surviving mainly among Roman Catholics, in honour of the many saints of this name.

variants.name: Gerrard
meanings: Variant spelling of   Gerard  , in part from the surname derived from the given name in the Middle Ages.

variants.name: Jerrard
meanings: Rare variant spelling of   Gerard  , probably influenced by the form of the modern surname, if not a transferred use.

nameMeanings.name: IV
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Short form of   Violet  ,   Vivien  , or   Vivian .

variants.name: Vivien
meanings: Earlier generally taken as a variant of the boy's name Vivian , but now also rarely used for boys. This spelling was quite common in Old French. Its use as a girl's name in the English-speaking world was influenced by Tennyson's Merlin and Vivien ( 1859 ). This name, from Arthurian legend, may represent an altered form of a Celtic name (perhaps akin to the Irish Gaelic name Béibhinn ‘white lady’, pronounced ‘bee-veen’). The actress Vivien Leigh ( 1913–67 ) was christened Vivian.

variants.name: Violet
meanings: From the name of the flower (Old French violette, Late Latin violetta, a diminutive of viola). This was one of the earliest flower names to become popular in Britain, being used as early as 1700 and becoming well established in the 19th century, although it is now somewhat out of favour.

variants.name: Vivian
meanings: Originally a boy's name, from an Old French form of the Latin name Vivianus (probably a derivative of vivus ‘alive’), but now more frequent as a girl's name. The name was borne by a 5th-century bishop of Saintes in western France, remembered for protecting his people during the invasion of the Visigoths.

en.lifeSummary: When Gérard IV de Metz de Bouzonville was born in 0981, in France, his father, Adalbert II comte de Metz et de Saargau, was 22 and his mother, Judith von Öhningen, was 32. He married Gisèle about 1008. They were the parents of at least 6 sons and 4 daughters. He died on 4 April 1047, in Remiremont, Vosges, Lorraine, France, at the age of 66, and was buried in Bouzonville, Moselle, Lorraine, France.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Gérard IV de Metz de Bouzonville est né en 0981, à France, son père, Adalbert II comte de Metz et de Saargau, avait 22 ans et sa mère, Judith von Öhningen, avait 32 ans. Il a épousé Gisèle environ 1008. Ils ont eu au moins 6 fils et 4 filles. Il est décédé le 4 avril 1047, à Remiremont, Vosges, Lorraine, France, à l’âge de 66 ans, et a été enterré à Bouzonville, Moselle, Lorraine, France.

de.lifeSummary: Als Gérard IV de Metz de Bouzonville 0981 in Frankreich geboren wurde, war sein Vater Adalbert II comte de Metz et de Saargau 22 und seine Mutter Judith von Öhningen 32. Er hat um 1008 Gisèle geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 6 Söhne und 4 Töchter. Er starb am 4. April 1047 im Alter von 66 Jahren in Remiremont, Vosges, Lothringen, Frankreich und wurde in Bouzonville, Mosel, Lothringen, Frankreich begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Gérard IV de Metz de Bouzonville è nato il nel 0981 nel Francia, il suo padre, Adalbert II comte de Metz et de Saargau, aveva 22 anni e il suo madre, Judith von Öhningen, aveva 32 anni. Lui sposato Gisèle circa 1008. Erano genitori di almeno 6 figli e 4 figlie. Morì il 4 aprile 1047, nel Remiremont, Vosges, Lorraine, Francia, all'età di 66 anni, e fu sepolto a Bouzonville, Moselle, Lorraine, Francia.

ja.lifeSummary: Gérard IV de Metz de Bouzonvilleが0981年にフランスで生まれた時，父Adalbert II comte de Metz et de Saargauは22歳，母Judith von Öhningenは32歳であった。約1008年にGisèleと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも6人の息子と4人の娘の親であった。彼は1047年4月4日にフランスロレィヌボスジェズレミーレモントで66歳で死去し，フランスロレィヌモセルボウゾンヴィルにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Gérard IV de Metz de Bouzonville(이)가 0981년 프랑스에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Adalbert II comte de Metz et de Saargau(은)는 22세였고, 그의 어머니 Judith von Öhningen(은)는 32세였다. 그는 대략 1008년 Gisèle(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 여섯 명의 아들과 네 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1047년 4월 4일 프랑스 로레이인 보스그스 렘이어몽에서 66세의 나이로 사망했으며 프랑스 로레이인 모셀 부존빌에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Gérard IV de Metz de Bouzonville nasceu em 0981, em França, seu pai, Adalbert II comte de Metz et de Saargau, tinha 22 anos e sua mãe, Judith von Öhningen, tinha 32 anos. Ele casou-se com Gisèle aproximadamente 1008. Eles tiveram pelo menos 6 filhos e 4 filhas. Ele faleceu em 4 de abril de 1047, em Remiremont, Vosges, Lorraine, França, com 66 anos, e foi sepultado em Bouzonville, Moselle, Lorraine, França.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Gérard IV de Metz de Bouzonville родился в 0981 г. в местности Франция, его отцу по имени Adalbert II comte de Metz et de Saargau было 22 года, а матери, которую звали Judith von Öhningen, 32. Он женился на женщине по имени Gisèle около 1008 г. У них было по крайней мере 6 сыновей и 4 дочери. Он умер 4 апреля 1047 г. в местности ремиремонт, фосгесь, лорреаин, Франция в возрасте 66 лет и был похоронен в местности боузонвилл, моселл, лорреаин, Франция.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Gérard IV de Metz de Bouzonville nació en 0981, en Francia, su papá, Adalbert II comte de Metz et de Saargau, tenía 22 años y su mamá, Judith von Öhningen, tenía 32 años. Se casó con Gisèle aproximadamente 1008. Fueron padres de por lo menos 6 hijos y 4 hijas. Murió el 4 de abril de 1047, en Remiremont, Vosges, Lorraine, Francia, a la edad de 66 años, y fue sepultado en Bouzonville, Moselle, Lorraine, Francia.
